message:
fix: runtime correctness and thread-safety improvements across core modules

Address several correctness issues discovered during testing and review:

Sleep thread-safety (src/time/sleep.rs):
- Replace `Cell<bool>` with `AtomicBool` for the `polled` tracking field.
  Cell<bool> is !Sync, which prevented Sleep from being shared across
  threads. Since Sleep is used by the scheduler which may move futures
  between worker threads, Send+Sync is required. All reads/writes use
  Relaxed ordering since the field is purely diagnostic.

IO copy yield budget (src/io/copy.rs):
- Replace persistent `yield_counter` struct field with local `steps`
  variable in each poll() call. The old design accumulated the counter
  across poll invocations, meaning a future that was polled many times
  could hit the yield budget prematurely on subsequent polls even if it
  hadn't done any work yet in that invocation. The local counter resets
  to zero on each poll entry, correctly measuring work-per-poll.
- Applied consistently to Copy, CopyBuf, CopyWithProgress, and
  CopyBidirectional.

Rate limiter wakeup registration (src/service/rate_limit.rs):
- When tokens are exhausted, instead of immediately calling
  wake_by_ref() (which causes a hot spin loop), create a Sleep timer
  targeting the next refill deadline. This lets the executor sleep
  until tokens actually become available.
- Add `sleep: Option<Sleep>` field to RateLimit, cleared on successful
  token acquisition, lazily created/reused on exhaustion.
- Add regression test `exhausted_tokens_register_waker_not_hang` that
  verifies the Sleep uses Sleep::new (not with_time_getter) so waker
  registration actually happens.

Scope panic propagation (src/cx/scope.rs):
- When a primary task is cancelled and we drain/join it, propagate
  JoinError::Panicked instead of silently discarding it with `let _ =`.
  This applies to both the scheduler-backed path (full drain) and the
  no-scheduler fallback (single poll). Silently swallowing panics makes
  debugging extremely difficult.

Combinator error messages (src/combinator/hedge.rs, race.rs):
- Change "winner panicked" to "branch panicked" in HedgeError::Panicked
  and RaceError::Panicked Display impls. The panicking branch may not be
  the winner; using "branch" is more accurate.

Select test fix (src/combinator/select.rs):
- Correct the test comment and assertion: left-biased Select returns A's
  result immediately without ever polling B. The previous comment
  incorrectly stated B was polled once.

Sync whitespace (src/sync/notify.rs, notify_lost_bug_test.rs):
- Trim trailing whitespace introduced in a previous commit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
